title: FHIR Ingestion
description: FHIR Ingestion converts FHIR R4 resources into the OMOP CDM format and loads them into your database. You can upload a single FHIR Bundle JSON file or batch-process multiple NDJSON files. The system maps FHIR resources (Patient, Condition, MedicationRequest, etc.) to the corresponding OMOP CDM tables and reports detailed per-table row counts.
tips: Upload a FHIR R4 Bundle JSON file using the file upload area, or drag and drop for convenience.
For large datasets, use the batch mode to upload multiple NDJSON files which are processed in parallel.
After ingestion, review the per-table breakdown showing how many rows were inserted into each CDM table (person, condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, etc.).
Check the health status indicator to verify the FHIR ingestion service is running before attempting an upload.
Ingestion history is stored locally so you can review past imports including timestamps, file names, and row counts.
Use the search and filter controls in the results panel to find specific FHIR resource types or CDM tables.
Failed or partially failed ingestions show detailed error information to help you identify and fix issues in your FHIR data.
Export the ingestion report for documentation or to track data lineage from FHIR source to OMOP CDM destination.
